denial_notice_text.statement: Denial reason: 'Request denied pending completion of conservative treatment. Physical therapy: 3 weeks completed, 6 required under standard pathway. Pharmacotherapy: one agent only. No interventional procedure on record.' Reason code: MED-003. Reviewer: Dr. Alan Foster, MD — HealthFirst Utilization Management.

imaging_findings.statement: C5-C6 moderate central cord compression. NO T2 SIGNAL CHANGE. NO MYELOMALACIA. Canal diameter 8mm. Cord contact present without compression.

imaging_reports.statement: C5-C6 moderate central cord compression. NO T2 SIGNAL CHANGE. NO MYELOMALACIA. Canal diameter 8mm. Cord contact present without compression.

imaging_dates.statement: MRI dated 2024-10-01 (within 6 months of appeal date 2024-11-18).

neurological_exam_findings.statement: Bilateral Hoffman signs present. Bilateral hyperreflexia mild. Tandem gait INTACT. Hand grip strength 4+/5 bilateral. No frank weakness. No dermatomal deficit. Activities of daily living FULLY INTACT per functional assessment.

neurological_exam_notes.statement: Bilateral Hoffman signs present. Bilateral hyperreflexia mild. Tandem gait INTACT. Hand grip strength 4+/5. No frank weakness. No dermatomal deficit. ADL intact.

functional_status.statement: mJOA score 15/18 (mild myelopathy category). ADL status: fully independent, no assistance required. Symptom duration: 6 months, STABLE.

functional_outcome_measures.statement: mJOA score 15/18. Mild myelopathy category. No validated outcome trending across PT visits documented.

symptoms_documented.statement: Mild myelopathic symptoms (bilateral Hoffman, mild hyperreflexia) localized to C5-C6 distribution. No dermatomal deficit. Tandem gait intact. ADL intact. 6-month stable course.

proposed_surgical_level.statement: ACDF C5-C6, one level. Matches imaging pathology level.

clinical_guidelines_applicability.statement: AANS/CNS 2023, NASS 2020, AO Spine 2021 govern. Mild myelopathy with mJOA 15, no myelomalacia, no acute progression, intact ADL classifies as Tier 2 (elective surgical candidate), not Tier 1 (urgent).

pt_records.statement: 3 weeks physical therapy (9 sessions). Symptoms remained stable during treatment. PT was halted by treating neurologist Dr. Osei citing myelopathy concern.

pt_discharge_summary.statement: PT was halted by Dr. Osei at 3 weeks. No formal PT discharge summary documenting functional plateau. PT notes describe symptoms as stable during treatment, not plateau in the sense of 'maximum therapeutic benefit reached.'

pt_progress_notes.statement: PT progress notes describe symptoms remaining stable (neither improving nor worsening) during the 3-week course.

pharmacotherapy_records.statement: ONE agent only: Baclofen 5mg BID for 6 weeks. Response: mild spasticity improvement. No second agent class documented.

interventional_records.statement: No interventional procedures documented. No epidural, no medial branch block, no trigger point injection.

conservative_treatment_history.statement: Conservative treatment summary: 3 weeks PT (halted by Dr. Osei); 6 weeks Baclofen (one agent only); no interventional. Both standard pathway (6 wks PT + 2 agents + 1 interventional) and myelopathy pathway (4 wks PT + 2 agents + interventional or waiver) remain unmet.

treating_physician_documentation.statement:
Dr. Osei letter states: 'This patient requires IMMEDIATE surgical intervention. The cervical cord compression is severe and progressive. Further delay is medically contraindicated. I cannot overstate the urgency — any further delay risks irreversible neurological deterioration. Immediate authorization is required.'

The letter is signed by Dr. Kwame Osei, MD, Neurology, the treating neurologist at Cedars-Sinai Medical Center. The letter does not provide objective findings supporting the urgency characterization beyond the diagnosis statement. Specifically, the letter does not document acute progressive deficit or cite myelomalacia.

Appeal basis stated: 'Treating neurologist declares surgical urgency and contraindication to further delay. Myelopathy confirmed on clinical exam. Conservative treatment has failed to arrest neurological progression.'
